North America Drone Market Forecast and Competitive Landscape Report 2025-2033 Featuring AeroVironment, Drone Delivery Canada, UVify, Intel, Alphabet, Boeing, Teledyne FLIR, 3D Robotics The North America Drone Market is projected to surge from US$ 11.20 billion in 2024 to US$ 26.90 billion by 2033, achieving a 10.22% CAGR from 2025. This growth is attributed to expanding commercial applications, defense investments, and favorable regulations. Sectors like agriculture, logistics, and public safety are leading drone adoption, with technologies enhancing connectivity through AI and IoT. The U.S. dominates due to its tech infrastructure, while Canada and Mexico show robust growth.

Intel's new CEO explores big shift in chip manufacturing business SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) -Intel's new chief executive is exploring a big change to its contract manufacturing business to win major customers, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters, in a potentially expensive shift from his predecessor's plans.  If implemented, the new strategy for what Intel calls its "foundry" business would entail no longer marketing certain chipmaking technology, which the company had long developed, to external customers, the people said.  Since taking the company's helm in March, CEO Lip-Bu Tan has moved fast to cut costs and find a new path to revive the ailing U.S. chipmaker.
